meta.title: Project detail template
meta.description: Reference layout for future IATF project detail pages.

hero.title: Project detail template
hero.lead: This route shows the structure that future public project pages should follow.
support: This file is a copy template for /projects/[slug] pages.

meta.label: Language notes
meta.value: Plain English source, translations may be added later

problem.title: What problem this project solves
paragraphs: Describe the accessibility problem in plain language.
Explain who is affected, what barrier exists today, and why the project matters.
This section should be understandable to a non-engineering audience.

why.title: Why this project exists
paragraphs: This project was accepted by IATF because it addresses a concrete accessibility need and can be built usefully in the open.
The goal is not just to create a private solution for one case. The goal is to publish work that others can inspect, reuse, improve, and learn from.

scope.title: Scope
paragraphs: This project currently focuses on the following:

status.title: Current status
paragraphs: Summarize the current state of the project. Examples:
items: looking for contributors or long-term maintainer
archived after reaching a natural stopping point

repository.title: Repository and development model
paragraphs: This project is developed in public in the IATF GitHub organization.
The repository is the main source of truth for code, issues, decisions, roadmap, and release history.
The assigned maintainer has repository-level responsibility, while IATF retains organizational ownership of the project space.

language.title: Language and accessibility notes
paragraphs: The public project description should remain clear and easy to understand.
If translated versions are added later, the plain English source should remain the main editorial reference unless the project explicitly says otherwise.
If language-specific support or contact routes exist, list them here.

use.title: How to use or evaluate it
paragraphs: If the project already has something usable, explain how to try it. Examples:
items: test against a documented use case

value.title: Accessibility and public value
paragraphs: Explain how this project contributes to accessibility in practice. Possible angles:
items: provides technical groundwork for future accessibility work

contributing.title: Contributing
paragraphs: Contributions are welcome unless the project page explicitly states otherwise.
Please check the repository README, contribution guidelines, and open issues before starting work.
If you are interested in helping but are unsure where to begin, open an issue or contact the maintainer through the repository.
